pcMetaCorrelations 0.1.0
  • Reference
  • Articles

pcMetaCorrelations AI Context

This repository is a separate R package project created to diagnose associations between PCA axes and metadata variables.

Purpose

  • Provide tools for identifying metadata variables that correlate with principal component axes.
  • Support both numeric and categorical metadata diagnostics.
  • Include plotting helpers so results can be inspected visually.

Current state

  • Package scaffold created in /Users/dryburghl/pcMetaCorrelations
  • Core implementation in R/pc_meta_correlations.R
  • Tests in tests/testthat/test-pc-meta-correlations.R
  • Package initialized as its own git repository

Key files

  • DESCRIPTION — package metadata and dependencies
  • NAMESPACE — exported functions
  • R/pc_meta_correlations.R — main implementation
  • README.md — overview and install example
  • tests/testthat/test-pc-meta-correlations.R — validation tests
  • .gitignore — ignore rules for R projects

Goals for the AI agent

  • Keep this package independent from the original weightedUMAP repo.
  • Focus on implementing and refining diagnostics for PC vs metadata associations.
  • Use devtools::load_all('.') and testthat::test_dir('tests/testthat') to validate changes.
  • Prefer minimal dependencies: base R, ggplot2, and Seurat only for optional Seurat object support.

Notes

  • This workspace should be treated as a separate repository and workflow.
  • If new functions are added, document them with roxygen comments and add tests.

Contents

Developed by Package Author.
